description: TraceAudit Style Guide — defines the canonical rules for each semantic bug category. The CoT Reviewer audits each execution trace against these rules.

RESOURCE_LEAK.description: A resource (file handle, socket, DB connection, lock) is acquired but not guaranteed to be released on all code paths, especially exceptions.
detection_signals: File opened without a context manager (with statement) or explicit .close() in a finally block
Socket or connection object created but no guarantee of .close() on exception paths
Threading Lock acquired via .acquire() without a corresponding .release() in a finally block
Generator or iterator opened but never exhausted or explicitly closed
Temporary file created but cleanup not guaranteed
trace_checklist: Identify every resource acquisition site (open, socket, connect, Lock.acquire)
Trace the EXCEPTION path — does the resource get released?
Trace the EARLY RETURN path — does the resource get released?
Check for nested resource acquisitions without proper nesting of finally blocks
RESOURCE_LEAK.linter_blind_spot: flake8/pylint cannot trace resource lifetime across function branches; they only flag missing 'with' for simple cases.
RESOURCE_LEAK.example_pattern: f = open(path); data = f.read(); return data  # f never closed on exception

BOUNDARY_VIOLATION.description: Off-by-one errors, incorrect slice indices, missing empty-collection guards, or wrong loop termination conditions that cause silent data corruption or IndexError.
detection_signals: Loop uses len(x) instead of len(x)-1 for index-based access
Slice uses [0:n] when [0:n+1] or [1:n] was intended
Function does not guard against empty list/string before indexing
Window/batch logic computes incorrect boundary at the last element
Off-by-one in pagination (0-indexed vs 1-indexed API mismatch)
trace_checklist: Manually evaluate the loop bounds for the FIRST and LAST iteration
Trace what happens when the input collection has 0 elements
Trace what happens when the input collection has exactly 1 element
Verify slice semantics: Python slices are [start:stop) — stop is exclusive
BOUNDARY_VIOLATION.linter_blind_spot: Static analysis cannot evaluate runtime index arithmetic without symbolic execution.
BOUNDARY_VIOLATION.example_pattern: for i in range(len(items)): process(items[i], items[i+1])  # IndexError on last item

INSECURE_DATA_FLOW.description: Sensitive data (passwords, tokens, keys) flows into an insecure sink (logs, URLs, plaintext files, error messages, or weak cryptographic operations).
detection_signals: Password or secret key passed to print(), logging.info(), or str()
Secret embedded in a URL query parameter (visible in server logs)
Hash comparison using == instead of hmac.compare_digest (timing attack)
Credentials stored as plaintext in a dict/file without encryption
Token/secret hardcoded in source code as a string literal
Sensitive exception traceback propagated to HTTP response body
trace_checklist: Label all variables that hold sensitive data (passwords, keys, tokens)
Trace each labeled variable to its eventual sink
Verify hash/signature comparisons use constant-time functions
Check that exception handlers do not re-raise with full tracebacks to callers
INSECURE_DATA_FLOW.linter_blind_spot: Bandit catches hardcoded secrets and some weak crypto, but misses tainted data flow through multiple function calls.
INSECURE_DATA_FLOW.example_pattern: logger.info(f'Auth attempt: user={user}, password={password}')  # credential leak

CONCURRENCY_HAZARD.description: A shared mutable resource is accessed from multiple threads without proper synchronization, causing race conditions, lost updates, or deadlocks.
detection_signals: Shared list/dict/counter mutated inside a thread without a Lock
read-modify-write sequence (x = x + 1) on a shared variable outside a Lock
Lock acquired in one order in Thread A and the reverse order in Thread B (deadlock)
queue.Queue used correctly for data but result collection list is mutated without a lock
Thread started with daemon=False but not joined, causing premature resource cleanup
trace_checklist: Identify all shared mutable state between threads
For each mutation site, check: is it inside a with lock: block?
Trace the lock acquisition ORDER across all code paths for deadlock potential
Verify producer/consumer queues use sentinel values correctly for clean shutdown
CONCURRENCY_HAZARD.linter_blind_spot: Thread safety cannot be verified by syntax-level analysis; it requires understanding of execution interleaving.
CONCURRENCY_HAZARD.example_pattern: results.append(x)  # results shared between threads, no lock around append

IMPROPER_ERROR_HANDLING.description: Exceptions are swallowed silently, caught too broadly, or handled in a way that masks the root cause and allows the program to continue in a corrupt state.
detection_signals: bare except: clause that catches all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt
except Exception: pass with no logging or re-raising
except block that only logs but allows execution to continue with invalid state
Return of a default value (None, [], 0) on exception without caller documentation
Exception caught and re-raised as a different type, losing the original traceback (raise NewError from None)
trace_checklist: Identify every try/except block
Trace what the program state looks like AFTER the except block executes
Verify: can the caller distinguish between a normal empty result and an error result?
Check if the exception type is specific enough to avoid catching unrelated errors
IMPROPER_ERROR_HANDLING.linter_blind_spot: Linters can flag bare 'except' and 'pass' but cannot assess whether swallowed exceptions lead to corrupt program state.
IMPROPER_ERROR_HANDLING.example_pattern:
try: result = process(data)
except Exception: result = []  # silent failure, caller unaware
